domain_package_coverage.coverage_signal: Strong — multiple series (kb52c, kb58, kb61, s24) covering haematocrit, MCH, MCHC, MCV, RBC, RDW, ferritin, transferrin, B12, folate.
Moderate — TSH, FT3, FT4, TPO-Ab, TgAb covered. Signal safety constraints (FT3 low, context-dependent patterns) must be confirmed as present in package content by Cursor.

Moderate — eGFR (2 packages), creatinine, urea, albumin present. ACR not found in package list; Cursor should confirm in biosmarkers.yaml and record honestly if absent. Non-diagnostic safety wording must be verified in package content.

changes.note: Standard SOP §10 HIGH triggers do not apply to a docs-only discovery sprint (no Intelligence Core, no SSOT, no analytical code). HIGH is conservative but safe. It is consistent with the merge control specified in the prompt ('Do not merge until GPT architectural review and human approval' — automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:525). Not a blocker.
changes.action: Accept HIGH — no correction required. Overhead is review burden only, not safety risk.

changes.detail: No backend/ssot/, backend/core/pipeline/, backend/core/analytics/, run_work_package.py, golden_gate_local.py, update_cursor_status.py, signal evaluator, root cause compiler, assembler, or any runtime file targeted for modification. All backend paths listed in prompt are READ targets for mapping evidence only.
No .codex/, .vscode/, AGENTS.md or tooling artefact risk. Only two docs/ files and bus lifecycle files in expected changed set.

All shell commands use PowerShell-compatible syntax: git switch, git pull, git status --short, git switch -c, git diff --stat, git diff --name-only. No bash-only constructs (&&, /dev/null, etc.) present.

Three launch-core domains (blood/iron/oxygen, thyroid/energy regulation, kidney function) are confirmed absent from existing domain implementation. Sprint has real discovery and mapping work to perform. Not a no-op.

changes.note: Prompt §Clinical safety discipline (lines 426-433) explicitly requires flagging safety/caution for thyroid, renal, anaemia, iron, oxygen signals. Cursor must not recommend activation without source support and must record medical review dependencies. FT3 low constraints and context-dependent thyroid patterns require explicit safety notes in the map. Non-diagnostic CKD language constraint in §7 is correctly scoped. These are execution requirements for Cursor — not hardening blockers.
changes.action: No change required. Safety discipline is well-specified in the prompt.

overall_verdict: HARDENED — all front matter fields confirmed (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:2-6). All 7 required authority paths verified to exist. All 12 supporting reference paths verified to exist. Package count confirmed at 187 (upper bound of 186/187 per strategy). Domain package coverage verified: blood/iron/oxygen (31 packages), thyroid/energy regulation (13 packages), kidney function (11 packages). change_type: CONTENT correct for docs-only sprint. Stage 2B NOT_APPLICABLE. STOP conditions explicit and deterministic (lines 31-44, 502-513). No Intelligence Core exposure. No tooling leakage risk. Three non-blocking advisories: (1) HIGH risk is conservative but consistent with merge controls; (2) r1 LAYER_ARCHITECTURE_AUTHORITY_INDEX co-exists — Cursor must use r2 only; (3) clinical safety discipline for thyroid/renal/anaemia signals must be observed during mapping. Cursor may proceed.
